message:
feat(undo-manager): add apply_operator command type for operator undo/redo

Extended the session undo/redo system to support operator application
commands, enabling users to undo and redo operator results (level-split,
exclusion-test, object-transpose, scale-check) during a Brenner Loop
research session.

New types and interfaces:
- CommandType: Added "apply_operator" to the union
- ApplyOperatorData: New interface capturing:
  - operatorType: Which operator was applied (camelCase or snake_case)
  - result: The operator's output data (typed as unknown for flexibility)
  - newPhase: Optional phase transition on execute
  - previousPhase: Optional phase to restore on undo

New functions:
- isOperatorCommand(): Type guard for operator commands
- normalizeOperatorApplicationKey(): Maps both naming conventions
  (levelSplit/level_split) to the Session.operatorApplications key
- createApplyOperatorCommand(): Factory function to create operator
  commands with proper execute/undo data

Updated applyCommand() case handling:
- Adds operator result to appropriate operatorApplications array
- Optionally transitions session phase on execute

Updated reverseCommand() case handling:
- Removes last result from operatorApplications array (stack pop)
- Restores previous phase on undo

This enables the session UI to provide a consistent undo experience
across all session mutations, including the guided operator wizards.
Users can now undo an accidental operator application and restore
the session to its previous state.
